svg.aria-label: Maglev load balancer coverage across ATOMOS ecosystem
svg.text: ATOMOS Maglev Load Balancer Coverage
Implemented ring-hash and Maglev-derived routing paths across ingress, data reads, and internal control-plane traffic
Edge Ingress Path
Global External HTTP(S) Load Balancer
Configured with HEADER_FIELD session affinity and RING_HASH policy.
Consistent hash key: X-Supplier-Id header.
Behavior: stable supplier-to-pod routing under scale events.
Source: infra/terraform/networking.tf
Policy: RING_HASH + HEADER
Hash header: X-Supplier-Id
Data Read Path
Spanner Read Router (Maglev-derived)
Pre-built H3 parent-cell lookup table for O(1) regional selection.
Zero coordination routing decision, deterministic table per pod.
Current runtime mode in bootstrap: single-region fallback.
Source: apps/backend-go/bootstrap/spannerrouter/router.go
Pattern: lookup-table routing
Current mode: NewSingleRegion
Internal RPC Path
Optimizer gRPC via xDS Service Mesh
Backend can dial xds:///ai-worker when OPTIMIZER_GRPC_ADDR is set.
Mesh control-plane applies balancing and retry policy.
This path is load-balanced, but explicit ring-hash policy is external.
Sources: optimizergrpc client and ai-worker gRPC server
xDS target: xds:///ai-worker
Env gate: OPTIMIZER_GRPC_ADDR
Coverage status across ecosystem surfaces
1) Ring-hash Maglev behavior is implemented at backend ingress infrastructure (not at web/mobile client layer).
2) Maglev-derived read-router code exists in backend, but runtime is currently single-region unless multiregion activation is wired in boot.
3) Internal xDS optimizer traffic is load-balanced; explicit Maglev/ring-hash policy is not declared in current app code.
4) Warehouse sibling reroute logic is operational load balancing and should not be conflated with Maglev ring-hash routing.
Authoritative code paths: infra/terraform/networking.tf, bootstrap/spannerrouter/router.go, bootstrap/new.go, internal/rpc/optimizergrpc/client.go, ai-worker/grpc_server.go.
Diagram intent: make implemented Maglev and Maglev-derived paths explicit, including activation boundaries.
